violations.text: Small glass reach-in at the bar was observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Thermometers are lacking in a couple of the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in the storage area. No foods observed to be contaminated. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Areas of droppings must be properly cleaned in order to check for new activity. Pest control is to be increased as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the lowboy unit observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Food debris observed in the hand-washing sink. An employee observed rinsing a pan in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service call to be placed. Sanitizer bay of the 3-bay sink was set up during inspection. Sanitizing bay must be used to sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and at the proper concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

Food contact surface of equipment/utensils (knife) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024
Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies was not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024
Test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher have expired. New test strips are to be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2024
The floor under the 3-bay sink is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2023

The wall/plumbing pipes/floor in the dishwashing area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
